channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Pennsylvania town grapples with Trump assassination attempt ahead of his return
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Donald Trump is going back to Butler, where the world last saw him pump his fist and beseech followers to “fight,” even as blood streaked his face from a would-be assassin's bullet.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Memorable moments from the vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) clashed Tuesday in what is likely to be the last debate of this presidential election season.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, ABC 4

item.title: Trump rejects calls for another debate with Harris after VP showdown
item.description: Former President Donald Trump again rejected the prospect of another debate with Vice President Kamala Harris late Tuesday following a showdown between the two vice presidential nominees, saying he was "not looking to do it again.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Wright Brothers Memorial plane crash victims identified, include 6-year-old child
item.description: Wright Brothers National Memorial have released the names of the adult victims killed when a single-engine plane crashed near the airport on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cadaver dogs wade through muck of communities 'wiped off the map' by Helene
item.description: Cadaver dogs and search crews trudged through knee-deep muck and debris in the mountains of western North Carolina
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Are LGBTQ Jews welcome in Orthodox communities? This is how they are building spaces of their own
item.description: It was a heart-wrenching choice. But when Daniel Gammerman decided to never set foot back in an Orthodox synagogue, he thought of it as an act of love. Not toward the Jewish community he was born into, but to himself.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Push to map Great Lakes bottom gains momentum amid promises effort will help fishing and shipping
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Jennifer Boehme grew up scouting beaches around her home in St. Petersburg, Florida, for whatever she could find. Rocks, sand dollars, coquina mollusks — anything the ocean gave up. Now, 40 years later, Boehme wants to launch another treasure hunt.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harris and Biden are fanning out across the Southeast as devastation from Helene grows
item.description: For the last four years, President Joe Biden has jetted off to survey damage and console Americans hit by tornadoes, wildfires and tropical storms.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vance called Harris a 'border czar' during the VP debate. What does that mean?
item.description: Republicans, including former President Donald Trump and his 2024 running mate, Senator J.D. Vance of Ohio, have increasingly referred to Vice President Kamala Harris as a failed "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KTLA 5

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: The Latest: Kirk becomes a hurricane as North Carolina reels from Helene
item.description: Desperate residents of the storm-battered mountains of western North Carolina lined up for water and food, hunted for cellphone signals and slogged buckets from creeks to flush toilets days after Hurricane Helene’s remnants deluged the region.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WWII destroyer found off California coast 76 years later
item.description: A surviving ship from WWII that was used by both Allied and Axis forces in the Pacific and that was given a ceremonial burial at sea has finally been located after more than seven decades underwater.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Nobert, Fox 5

item.title: LIVE UPDATES: Vance, Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees J.D. Vance and Tim Walz are facing off for the first -- and likely only -- time before the 2024 election, which is just over a month away.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WWII destroyer found off California coast 76 years later
item.description: A surviving ship from WWII that was used by both Allied and Axis forces in the Pacific and that was given a ceremonial burial at sea has finally been located after more than seven decades underwater.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Nobert, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC mayor deflects questions about bribery charges as a potential witness speaks outside City Hall
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams delivered his most thorough public defense to date since his indictment on federal bribery charges, insisting the case was a “test” that would not distract him from governing as he deflected specific questions about the investigation for more than an ho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rejects computer repairman's defamation claims over reports on Hunter Biden laptop
item.description: DOVER, Del. (AP) — A computer repairman at the center of a controversy over Hunter Biden's laptop has lost his defamation case against news outlets, the president's son and Joe Biden's presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: John Amos, patriarch on 'Good Times' and an Emmy nominee for the blockbuster 'Roots,' dies at 84
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — John Amos, who starred as the family patriarch on the hit 1970s sitcom “Good Times" and earned an Emmy nomination for his role in the seminal 1977 miniseries “Roots,” has died. He was 84. He died Aug. 21 of natural causes in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP leaders press Biden administration to get tougher on Iran after missile attack
item.description: Republican leaders on Capitol Hill are calling on President Biden to get tougher on Iran in the wake of Tuesday’s missile attack on Israel, urging the administration to adopt a series of specific measures designed to cripple the military powers of Tehran and its proxies.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Medical examiner releases cause of Rich Homie Quan's death
item.description: At the time, some suspected Quan died of a drug overdose, a prediction that turned out to be accurate.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: The poll offers much better news for Trump than last month’s poll from Quinnipiac, which found Harris leading Trump by 3 points.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: McDonald’s is bringing back its famous ‘Boo Buckets,’ with a new color
item.description: McDonald's famous Boo Buckets are making a return just in time for Halloween, and a new color has joined the lineup this year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cook Political Report shifts Texas Senate race toward Democrats
item.description: The Cook Political Report on Tuesday shifted Texas’s Senate race from "likely Republican" to "lean Republican," signaling momentum for Rep. Colin Allred's (D) challenge against incumbent Sen. Ted Cruz (R) in the red state.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Education a potential flashpoint in Vance-Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Education hardly came up in last month’s Trump-Harris presidential debate, but it could be a central focus at Tuesday’s undercard event.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Montana man gets 6 months in prison for cloning giant sheep and breeding it
item.description: GREAT FALLS, Mont. (AP) — An 81-year-old Montana man was sentenced Monday to six months in federal prison for illegally using tissue and testicles from large sheep hunted in Central Asia and the U.S. to create hybrid sheep for captive trophy hunting in Texas and Minnesota. U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMY BETH HANSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Is the food in the fridge still good? California wants to end the guessing game
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California wants to help end the everyday household debate over whether the food in the fridge is still good to eat. Food labels that say “sell by” or “best before” are misleading because they have no universal meaning under current laws.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A chemical cloud moving around Atlanta's suburbs prompts a new shelter-in-place alert
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Residents east of Atlanta were again warned Tuesday to take shelter where they are if shifting winds push the still-billowing chemical cloud from a chlorine factory fire over their neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Habit Burger & Grill trolls rival In-N-Out
item.description: Habit Burger & Grill isn’t letting anyone, especially In-N-Out Burger, forget that it has the best fast-food burger in town—at least according to one survey.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, ABC 4

item.title: My car was submerged in floodwaters. Should I start it?
item.description: Storm surges and flooding from Hurricane Helene have caused widespread devastation across parts of the Southeastern U.S., in some cases leaving homes and cars completely underwater.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Helene's death toll rises to more than 130 as supplies are rushed to areas in need
item.description: A crisis unfolded in Asheville, North Carolina, as officials pledged to get more water, food and other supplies to flood-stricken areas without power and cellular service Monday, days after Hurricane Helene ripped across the U.S. Southeast. The death toll from the storm surpassed 130.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gavin Creel, Tony Award-winning veteran of Broadway musicals, dies at 48
item.description: Creel's publicist said he died at his home in Manhattan of metastatic melanotic peripheral nerve sheath sarcoma.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Fox 5

item.title: Oasis announce 3 US tour dates: Where and when are they playing?
item.description: Fans were expecting the band to schedule stops in North America after billboards teasing their reunion began popping up in U.S. and Canadian cities.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Helene's death toll rises to more than 100 as supplies rushed to isolated communities
item.description: A crisis unfolded in Asheville, North Carolina, as officials pledged to get more water, food and other supplies to flood-stricken areas without power and cellular service Monday, days after Hurricane Helene ripped across the U.S. Southeast. The death toll from the storm surpassed 100.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: John Ashton of 'Beverly Hills Cop' dies at 76
item.description: In a career that spanned more than 50 years, Ashton was a regular face across TV series and films, including "Midnight Run,” “Little Big League” and “Gone Baby Gone.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
